front: Composer: French Baroque composer who succeeded Lully as France's dominant opera composer; rose to fame with his 1722 Treatise on Harmony
Composer: Treatise on Harmony introduced the concept of 'fundamental bass' and proposed that chords can be inversions of a root, codifying tonal music theory
Composer: his operas were championed in the War of the Buffoons against fans of Pergolesi's La serva padrona; Jean-Jacques Rousseau opposed him
Composer: Debussy composed a piano homage to him in the first book of Images; Vikingur Ólafsson paired them as 'painters in sound'
Opera: tragedie en musique based on Racine's Phèdre; Rameau's operatic debut; criticized by Lully partisans as destroying the older style
Opera: Telaira sings the aria 'Tristes apprêts, pâles flambeaux'
Opera: twin brothers, only one initially immortal; Jupiter decrees they share immortality at the end
Opera: opera-ballet in four acts set in Turkey, Peru, Persia, and North America
Opera: North American act ('Les Sauvages') features Zima and Adario pledging love before a peace pipe ceremony
Opera: Incas of Peru entrée has rivals Don Carlos and Huascar court Princess Phani while a volcano erupts
Harpsichord piece: opening notes G-G-G-G-G-G-B-flat-D with 'co co co co co co co dai' written underneath, imitating a hen clucking
Harpsichord piece: the left hand constantly crosses over the right, creating the illusion of a physical deformity
back: The Three Hands (Rameau, Pièces de clavecin)

front: Composer: Voltaire wrote the libretto for his opera Le Temple de la Gloire
